rawText:
Beneath the optimistic macroeconomic headlines emanating from the continent — robust GDP growth, record bond issuances, surging foreign direct investment — lies a more complicated story of governance, one in which the entanglement of political power and economic privilege continues to distort markets, fuel conflict, and test whether African states can reform themselves before their contradictions become untenable.

Kenya's ailing sugar sector, in which 9 million people depend on an industry systematically hollowed out by politically connected "sugar barons" who extract vast rents through strict import licences and state-controlled prices, offers a microcosm of the broader challenge — a country whose solid headline figures, including nearly 5% GDP growth forecast for 2026, mask chronic low productivity and a corrosive enmeshing of politics and business that facilitates corruption at every level.

President William Ruto's government has taken tentative steps toward liberalisation, leasing inefficient state-owned mills to private investors and finally lifting restrictions on regional sugar imports after a 24-year delay, reforms that the World Bank estimates could, if extended to electricity, transport, and agribusiness, add nearly 1.4 percentage points to annual GDP growth and create more than 400,000 new jobs per year.

Yet sceptics have ample reason to doubt whether such reforms will survive contact with political reality, given that a Privatisation Act granting sweeping powers to the presidency has already produced opaque tendering processes in which all licences went to incumbents rather than foreign competitors — and with an election looming in 2027, the incentive to backtrack on any reform that reduces patronage opportunities grows stronger by the month.

Thousands of kilometres to the west, in the mountainous back country of South Kivu province, the war in eastern Congo — where the Rwanda-backed M23 militia captured Goma and Bukavu more than a year ago — is escalating far from the view of a world preoccupied with crises elsewhere, displacing half a million civilians since December alone while inflaming ethnic tensions rooted in atrocities committed during the Congo wars of the late 1990s.

The December peace deal brokered by America and signed by the presidents of Congo and Rwanda has proven hollow, as fighting spreads to ever more remote areas, raising the alarming prospect that M23 could push toward Kalemie and bring the conflict uncomfortably close to Katanga — Congo's industrial heartland and home to the very copper and cobalt mines that the American administration is courting Western firms to develop.

The Congo conflict illustrates a grim paradox of African development: that the same mineral wealth attracting unprecedented foreign investment can also fuel the armed groups and proxy wars that make governance impossible, a dynamic in which American sanctions on Rwandan officials and Angolan-brokered peace initiatives have thus far proven equally futile against combatants who see control of territory as control of revenue.

In east Africa's media landscape, the acquisition of the Nation Media Group — one of the continent's largest and most independent media empires — by Rostam Azizi, a Tanzanian industrialist with extensive business interests across the region, poses a different but related governance question: whether press freedom, long considered Kenya's democratic crown jewel, can survive ownership by a tycoon whose commercial ventures may depend on maintaining warm relations with increasingly autocratic governments that have already pressured journalists and barred reporters from covering official events.

Across these disparate arenas — the sugar fields of western Kenya, the besieged villages of South Kivu, and the newsrooms of Nairobi — a common thread emerges in the tension between Africa's genuine economic potential and the political systems that too often capture its benefits for elites, suppress its critics, and allow conflict to fester in resource-rich peripheries while capitals celebrate rising stock indices and record bond sales.

The unfinished business of African governance, therefore, is not a secondary concern to be addressed after prosperity arrives but rather the precondition upon which sustainable prosperity depends — for without institutions strong enough to resist patronage, press free enough to expose corruption, and peace durable enough to protect the displaced farmers and widowed families of eastern Congo, the continent's promising economic trajectory risks remaining, as it has so many times before, a story of potential perpetually deferred.

overview.title: Reform, Conflict, and the Unfinished Business of African Governance

sentences.original: Beneath the optimistic macroeconomic headlines emanating from the continent — robust GDP growth, record bond issuances, surging foreign direct investment — lies a more complicated story of governance, one in which the entanglement of political power and economic privilege continues to distort markets, fuel conflict, and test whether African states can reform themselves before their contradictions become untenable.
sentences.translation: 대륙에서 흘러나오는 낙관적인 거시경제 헤드라인 — 견조한 GDP 성장, 사상 최대 채권 발행, 급증하는 외국인 직접 투자 — 아래에는 정치 권력과 경제적 특권의 얽힘이 계속해서 시장을 왜곡하고, 분쟁을 부추기며, 아프리카 국가들이 모순이 견딜 수 없게 되기 전에 스스로를 개혁할 수 있는지를 시험하는, 더 복잡한 거버넌스 이야기가 놓여 있다.

grammarElements.text: a more complicated story of governance
grammarElements.label: 도치된 주어

grammarElements.text: one in which the entanglement ... continues to distort, fuel, and test
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절 + 삼중 병렬 동사

vocabulary.example: The entanglement of politics and business breeds corruption.

vocabulary.meaning: 유지할 수 없는, 견딜 수 없는
vocabulary.example: The government's position became untenable after the scandal.

expressions.usage: 정보·분위기·영향이 특정 출처에서 나올 때 사용하는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: Beneath [positive surface] lies a more complicated story of [deeper issue]
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 외양 아래 숨겨진 복잡한 현실을 드러내는 도치 도입 패턴
examples: Beneath the booming tech sector lies a more complicated story of inequality.
Beneath the diplomatic smiles lies a more complicated story of territorial ambition.

drillQuestions.question: Beneath the optimistic macroeconomic headlines ______ a more complicated story of governance.

sentences.notes: Beneath ... lies 도치 구조가 낙관적 수치 뒤의 거버넌스 문제를 극적으로 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Beneath the optimistic macroeconomic headlines
lies a more complicated story of governance
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 복잡한 거버넌스 이야기가 놓여 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the entanglement of political power and economic privilege
before their contradictions become untenable
koreanAlignment.ko: 모순이 견딜 수 없게 되기 전에

sentences.original: Kenya's ailing sugar sector, in which 9 million people depend on an industry systematically hollowed out by politically connected "sugar barons" who extract vast rents through strict import licences and state-controlled prices, offers a microcosm of the broader challenge — a country whose solid headline figures, including nearly 5% GDP growth forecast for 2026, mask chronic low productivity and a corrosive enmeshing of politics and business that facilitates corruption at every level.
sentences.translation: 정치적으로 연줄이 닿은 '설탕 남작들'이 엄격한 수입 허가와 국가 통제 가격을 통해 막대한 지대를 추출하면서 조직적으로 속이 비워진 산업에 900만 명이 의존하는 케냐의 병든 설탕 산업은 더 넓은 도전의 축소판을 보여준다 — 2026년 거의 5퍼센트의 GDP 성장 전망을 포함한 탄탄한 헤드라인 수치가 만성적인 저생산성과 모든 수준에서 부패를 촉진하는 정치와 사업의 부식적 얽힘을 가리는 나라이다.

grammarElements.text: in which 9 million people depend on an industry systematically hollowed out by ...
grammarElements.label: 전치사 + 관계절 수식

grammarElements.text: a microcosm of the broader challenge
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The village offers a microcosm of the nation's ethnic tensions.

expressions.usage: 제도나 산업이 내부에서부터 약화될 때 사용하는 비유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Specific case] offers a microcosm of the broader [challenge/trend]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 사례를 더 큰 현상의 축소판으로 제시하는 분석 패턴
examples: Detroit's decline offers a microcosm of the broader deindustrialisation of America.
The school's funding crisis offers a microcosm of the broader squeeze on public services.

drillQuestions.question: Kenya's ailing sugar sector offers a ______ of the broader challenge.

sentences.notes: sugar barons라는 비유적 명칭이 정경유착의 핵심 행위자를 한마디로 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Kenya's ailing sugar sector
systematically hollowed out by politically connected 'sugar barons'
koreanAlignment.ko: 정치적 연줄의 '설탕 남작들'에 의해 조직적으로 공동화된

koreanAlignment.en: offers a microcosm of the broader challenge
a corrosive enmeshing of politics and business

sentences.original: President William Ruto's government has taken tentative steps toward liberalisation, leasing inefficient state-owned mills to private investors and finally lifting restrictions on regional sugar imports after a 24-year delay, reforms that the World Bank estimates could, if extended to electricity, transport, and agribusiness, add nearly 1.4 percentage points to annual GDP growth and create more than 400,000 new jobs per year.
sentences.translation: 윌리엄 루토 대통령 정부는 자유화를 향해 조심스러운 조치를 취해왔는데, 비효율적인 국영 제당소를 민간 투자자에게 임대하고 24년간의 지연 끝에 마침내 지역 설탕 수입 제한을 해제했다. 세계은행은 이러한 개혁이 전력, 교통, 농업까지 확대되면 연간 GDP 성장에 거의 1.4퍼센트포인트를 더하고 매년 40만 개 이상의 새 일자리를 창출할 수 있다고 추정한다.

grammarElements.text: reforms that the World Bank estimates could ... add ... and create
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절 + 삽입절

vocabulary.example: The government has taken only tentative steps toward reform.

expressions.usage: 개혁이 아직 과감하지 않고 탐색적 단계임을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Government] has taken tentative steps toward [reform], [verb]-ing [A] and [verb]-ing [B]
patterns.explanation: 정부의 조심스러운 개혁 행보를 구체적 조치와 함께 서술하는 패턴
examples: The ministry has taken tentative steps toward deregulation, easing licensing rules and opening markets to competition.
The central bank has taken tentative steps toward tightening, raising rates and reducing bond purchases.

drillQuestions.question: President Ruto's government has taken ______ steps toward liberalisation.

sentences.notes: tentative라는 단어 선택이 개혁의 불완전성과 정치적 제약을 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: has taken tentative steps toward liberalisation
leasing inefficient state-owned mills to private investors
koreanAlignment.ko: 비효율적인 국영 제당소를 민간 투자자에게 임대하고

koreanAlignment.en: after a 24-year delay
add nearly 1.4 percentage points to annual GDP growth

sentences.original: Yet sceptics have ample reason to doubt whether such reforms will survive contact with political reality, given that a Privatisation Act granting sweeping powers to the presidency has already produced opaque tendering processes in which all licences went to incumbents rather than foreign competitors — and with an election looming in 2027, the incentive to backtrack on any reform that reduces patronage opportunities grows stronger by the month.
sentences.translation: 그러나 회의론자들이 그러한 개혁이 정치적 현실과 만나서도 살아남을지 의심할 충분한 이유가 있다. 대통령직에 광범위한 권한을 부여한 민영화법이 이미 모든 허가가 외국 경쟁자가 아닌 기존 사업자에게 돌아간 불투명한 입찰 과정을 만들어냈기 때문이며, 2027년 선거가 다가오면서 후원 기회를 줄이는 어떤 개혁이든 뒤집으려는 유인이 달마다 강해지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: whether such reforms will survive contact with political reality
grammarElements.label: whether 간접의문문 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: given that a Privatisation Act ... has already produced
grammarElements.label: given that 이유절

expressions.usage: 이상적 계획이 현실에 부딪혔을 때 유지될 수 있는지를 시험할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Sceptics] have ample reason to doubt whether [reform] will survive [reality]
patterns.explanation: 개혁의 실현 가능성에 대한 회의를 제시하면서 근거를 뒤따르게 하는 패턴
examples: Critics have ample reason to doubt whether the ceasefire will survive the next provocation.
Analysts have ample reason to doubt whether the merger will survive regulatory scrutiny.

drillQuestions.question: Sceptics have ample reason to doubt whether such reforms will survive contact with political ______.

sentences.notes: survive contact with political reality라는 표현이 전장의 격언('계획은 적과 만나면 살아남지 못한다')을 연상시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: sceptics have ample reason to doubt
survive contact with political reality
opaque tendering processes
the incentive to backtrack grows stronger by the month

sentences.original: Thousands of kilometres to the west, in the mountainous back country of South Kivu province, the war in eastern Congo — where the Rwanda-backed M23 militia captured Goma and Bukavu more than a year ago — is escalating far from the view of a world preoccupied with crises elsewhere, displacing half a million civilians since December alone while inflaming ethnic tensions rooted in atrocities committed during the Congo wars of the late 1990s.
sentences.translation: 수천 킬로미터 서쪽, 남키부 주의 산악 오지에서 동부 콩고 전쟁은 — 르완다가 지원하는 M23 민병대가 1년 전 고마와 부카부를 점령한 곳 — 다른 곳의 위기에 몰두해 있는 세계의 시야에서 멀리 떨어진 채 격화되고 있으며, 12월 이래로만 50만 명의 민간인을 이주시키는 한편 1990년대 말 콩고 전쟁 당시 자행된 잔학행위에 뿌리를 둔 민족 갈등을 격화시키고 있다.

grammarElements.text: where the Rwanda-backed M23 militia captured Goma and Bukavu
grammarElements.label: 삽입 관계절 (배경 정보)

vocabulary.example: The international community remains preoccupied with conflicts in the Middle East.

expressions.usage: 사건이 관심 밖에서 진행되고 있음을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Conflict] is escalating far from the view of a world preoccupied with [other crises]
patterns.explanation: 세계의 관심 밖에서 격화되는 분쟁을 부각하는 패턴
examples: The humanitarian crisis is escalating far from the view of a world preoccupied with trade wars.
The famine is worsening far from the view of a world preoccupied with pandemic recovery.

drillQuestions.question: The war in eastern Congo is escalating far from the view of a world ______ with crises elsewhere.

sentences.notes: 지리적 전환('Thousands of kilometres to the west')이 독자를 케냐에서 콩고로 이동시키는 장면 전환 장치로 기능한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Thousands of kilometres to the west
the war in eastern Congo is escalating
far from the view of a world preoccupied with crises elsewhere
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 곳의 위기에 몰두해 있는 세계의 시야에서 멀리

koreanAlignment.en: displacing half a million civilians since December alone

sentences.original: The December peace deal brokered by America and signed by the presidents of Congo and Rwanda has proven hollow, as fighting spreads to ever more remote areas, raising the alarming prospect that M23 could push toward Kalemie and bring the conflict uncomfortably close to Katanga — Congo's industrial heartland and home to the very copper and cobalt mines that the American administration is courting Western firms to develop.
sentences.translation: 미국이 중재하고 콩고와 르완다 대통령이 서명한 12월 평화 협정은 공허한 것으로 드러났다. 전투가 점점 더 외진 지역으로 확산되면서 M23이 칼레미에 방향으로 진격하여 분쟁을 카탕가 — 콩고의 산업적 심장부이자 미국 행정부가 서방 기업에 개발을 권유하는 바로 그 구리와 코발트 광산의 본거지 — 에 불편할 정도로 가까이 가져올 수 있다는 우려스러운 전망이 제기되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The December peace deal brokered by America and signed by the presidents
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 이중 과거분사 수식

grammarElements.text: as fighting spreads to ever more remote areas
grammarElements.label: as 이유/상황절

vocabulary.example: The ceasefire agreement proved hollow within weeks.

expressions.usage: 부정적 시나리오의 가능성을 경고할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Agreement] has proven hollow, as [negative development] raises the prospect that [worse outcome]
patterns.explanation: 합의의 실패를 선언한 뒤 악화 가능성을 연결하는 위기 서술 패턴
examples: The truce has proven hollow, as shelling resumes and raises the prospect that the capital could fall.
The trade deal has proven hollow, as tariffs persist and raise the prospect that supply chains will decouple entirely.

drillQuestions.question: The December peace deal brokered by America has proven ______.

sentences.notes: the very copper and cobalt mines에서 very는 '바로 그'라는 강조 용법으로, 전쟁과 투자가 같은 자원을 둘러싸고 있음을 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The December peace deal has proven hollow
fighting spreads to ever more remote areas
koreanAlignment.ko: 전투가 점점 더 외진 지역으로 확산되면서

koreanAlignment.en: Congo's industrial heartland
the very copper and cobalt mines

sentences.original: The Congo conflict illustrates a grim paradox of African development: that the same mineral wealth attracting unprecedented foreign investment can also fuel the armed groups and proxy wars that make governance impossible, a dynamic in which American sanctions on Rwandan officials and Angolan-brokered peace initiatives have thus far proven equally futile against combatants who see control of territory as control of revenue.
sentences.translation: 콩고 분쟁은 아프리카 발전의 암울한 역설을 보여준다 — 전례 없는 외국 투자를 끌어들이는 바로 그 광물 자원이 거버넌스를 불가능하게 만드는 무장 집단과 대리전에도 연료를 공급할 수 있다는 것이다. 르완다 관리에 대한 미국의 제재와 앙골라가 중재한 평화 이니셔티브 모두 영토 장악을 수입 장악으로 보는 전투원들에게는 지금까지 동등하게 무력했다.

grammarElements.text: a grim paradox of African development
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: that the same mineral wealth ... can also fuel ...
grammarElements.label: that 동격절 (역설의 내용)

grammarElements.text: a dynamic in which ... have proven equally futile
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Diplomatic efforts have proven futile against entrenched warlords.

expressions.usage: 한 현상이 서로 모순되는 두 결과를 동시에 낳을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Case] illustrates a grim paradox: that the same [asset] attracting [positive] can also fuel [negative]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 자원이 긍정적 결과와 부정적 결과를 동시에 낳는 역설을 제시하는 패턴
examples: Oil illustrates a grim paradox: that the same resource attracting billions in investment can also fuel authoritarian regimes.
Social media illustrates a grim paradox: that the same platform connecting communities can also fuel misinformation.

drillQuestions.question: American sanctions and Angolan-brokered peace initiatives have thus far proven equally ______.

sentences.notes: the same ... can also 구조가 광물 자원의 이중성 — 투자 유인이자 분쟁 연료 — 을 한 문장으로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: illustrates a grim paradox of African development
the same mineral wealth
make governance impossible
control of territory as control of revenue

sentences.original: In east Africa's media landscape, the acquisition of the Nation Media Group — one of the continent's largest and most independent media empires — by Rostam Azizi, a Tanzanian industrialist with extensive business interests across the region, poses a different but related governance question: whether press freedom, long considered Kenya's democratic crown jewel, can survive ownership by a tycoon whose commercial ventures may depend on maintaining warm relations with increasingly autocratic governments that have already pressured journalists and barred reporters from covering official events.
sentences.translation: 동아프리카의 미디어 지형에서, 대륙 최대이자 가장 독립적인 언론 제국 중 하나인 네이션 미디어 그룹을 지역 전반에 걸쳐 광범위한 사업 이해관계를 가진 탄자니아 기업인 로스탐 아지지가 인수한 것은 다르지만 관련된 거버넌스 질문을 던진다 — 오래도록 케냐 민주주의의 왕관 보석으로 여겨져 온 언론 자유가, 이미 기자들에게 압력을 가하고 공식 행사 취재를 금지한 점점 더 독재적인 정부들과 우호 관계 유지에 사업이 의존할 수 있는 거물의 소유 아래서 살아남을 수 있는가 하는 질문이다.

grammarElements.text: the acquisition of the Nation Media Group ... by Rostam Azizi
grammarElements.label: 주어 (of/by 수식 포함)

grammarElements.text: a different but related governance question
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: whether press freedom ... can survive ownership by a tycoon whose ...
grammarElements.label: whether 동격절 (질문의 내용)

vocabulary.example: Increasingly autocratic governments pose threats to press freedom.

expressions.meaning: 민주주의의 왕관 보석, 가장 소중한 민주적 성취
expressions.usage: 어떤 제도가 민주주의의 상징으로 간주될 때 사용하는 비유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Event] poses a [type] governance question: whether [value] can survive [threat]
patterns.explanation: 특정 사건이 제도적 가치의 존속 여부를 시험하는 질문을 던진다고 서술하는 패턴
examples: The merger poses a critical governance question: whether editorial independence can survive corporate ownership.
The appointment poses a troubling governance question: whether judicial independence can survive political interference.

drillQuestions.question: The acquisition poses a different but related governance ______.

sentences.notes: crown jewel이라는 비유가 언론 자유를 케냐 민주주의에서 가장 귀한 것으로 격상시킨 뒤, 그 위협을 질문형으로 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the acquisition of the Nation Media Group
poses a different but related governance question
Kenya's democratic crown jewel
increasingly autocratic governments

sentences.original: Across these disparate arenas — the sugar fields of western Kenya, the besieged villages of South Kivu, and the newsrooms of Nairobi — a common thread emerges in the tension between Africa's genuine economic potential and the political systems that too often capture its benefits for elites, suppress its critics, and allow conflict to fester in resource-rich peripheries while capitals celebrate rising stock indices and record bond sales.
sentences.translation: 이 서로 다른 무대들 — 서부 케냐의 사탕수수 밭, 남키부의 포위된 마을들, 나이로비의 뉴스룸 — 을 가로질러 공통된 맥락이 떠오른다. 아프리카의 진정한 경제적 잠재력과, 너무 자주 그 혜택을 엘리트를 위해 독점하고, 비판자를 탄압하며, 수도가 상승하는 주가지수와 사상 최대 채권 매출을 자축하는 동안 자원이 풍부한 주변부에서 분쟁이 곪도록 방치하는 정치 체제 사이의 긴장이 그것이다.

grammarElements.text: in the tension between ... and the political systems that ... capture, suppress, and allow
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 삼중 병렬 관계절

vocabulary.example: Unresolved tensions were allowed to fester for decades.

expressions.usage: 여러 사례를 관통하는 공통점을 도출할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Across these disparate arenas, a common thread emerges in the tension between [A] and [B]
patterns.explanation: 서로 다른 사례들을 관통하는 공통 긴장을 종합하는 패턴
examples: Across these disparate cases, a common thread emerges in the tension between innovation and regulation.
Across these different sectors, a common thread emerges in the tension between efficiency and equity.

drillQuestions.question: A common ______ emerges in the tension between Africa's potential and its political systems.

sentences.notes: 세 개의 구체적 장소(설탕밭, 마을, 뉴스룸)가 기사 전체를 한 줄로 꿰는 비유적 요약이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Across these disparate arenas
a common thread emerges
capture its benefits for elites
allow conflict to fester in resource-rich peripheries
koreanAlignment.ko: 자원이 풍부한 주변부에서 분쟁이 곪도록 방치하는

pronunciationNotes: disparate /ˈdɪspərət/ — '디스퍼럿', 첫 음절 강세, desperate와 혼동 주의

sentences.original: The unfinished business of African governance, therefore, is not a secondary concern to be addressed after prosperity arrives but rather the precondition upon which sustainable prosperity depends — for without institutions strong enough to resist patronage, press free enough to expose corruption, and peace durable enough to protect the displaced farmers and widowed families of eastern Congo, the continent's promising economic trajectory risks remaining, as it has so many times before, a story of potential perpetually deferred.
sentences.translation: 따라서 아프리카 거버넌스의 미완의 과제는 번영이 도착한 후에 다룰 부차적 관심사가 아니라, 지속 가능한 번영이 의존하는 전제 조건이다 — 후원에 저항할 만큼 강한 제도, 부패를 폭로할 만큼 자유로운 언론, 동부 콩고의 실향민 농부와 미망인 가족을 보호할 만큼 튼튼한 평화 없이는, 대륙의 유망한 경제적 궤적은 이전에 그토록 많이 그래왔듯 영원히 유보된 잠재력의 이야기로 남을 위험이 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The unfinished business of African governance
grammarElements.label: 주어
not A but rather B 구조

grammarElements.text: the precondition upon which sustainable prosperity depends
grammarElements.label: 보어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: for without institutions ... press ... and peace ...
grammarElements.label: for 이유절 + without 삼중 병렬

vocabulary.example: Good governance is the precondition for lasting economic growth.
The promise of prosperity has been perpetually deferred.

expressions.usage: 실현되지 못한 채 반복적으로 미뤄지는 가능성을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Issue] is not a secondary concern but rather the precondition upon which [goal] depends
patterns.explanation: 흔히 부차적으로 여겨지는 문제가 사실은 핵심 전제조건임을 강조하는 마무리 패턴
examples: Cybersecurity is not a secondary concern but rather the precondition upon which digital commerce depends.
Mental health is not a secondary concern but rather the precondition upon which workforce productivity depends.

drillQuestions.question: The unfinished business of African governance is not a secondary concern but rather the ______ upon which sustainable prosperity depends.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문장이 without 이하 세 가지 조건(제도·언론·평화)을 병렬로 나열하며 기사 전체의 세 소주제를 회수한다. 'potential perpetually deferred'는 아프리카에 자주 붙는 꼬리표를 의도적으로 반복하며 경고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The unfinished business of African governance
not a secondary concern but rather the precondition
institutions strong enough to resist patronage
a story of potential perpetually deferred
